title: Full-Length Plate Rack
summary: A tall wall-mounted plate rack with seven evenly spaced slots, built entirely from 1x4 and 1x2 stock — display plates upright rather than stacked flat.
description:
Two long 1x4 side rails run the full height of the rack, with seven 1x2 slats notched between them to form the individual plate slots and six 1x4 crosspieces reinforcing the structure between slots — the slot spacing is what determines how many plates fit and how they lean, so keep the gaps consistent from top to bottom.

**Don't build this wider than the plan without checking span** — shelves longer than about 36" start to sag under the weight of a full row of stacked plates, so if a wider rack is wanted, add a center support rather than just stretching the slats.

The height and shelf spacing can both be adjusted from the plan's dimensions to suit a specific run of plates, since this is fundamentally a repeating slot pattern rather than a fixed-size case. Mount securely to a wall stud — a fully loaded plate rack is heavier than it looks.

materials.name: Brad nails, 1-1/4", or self-tapping star bit screws, 1-1/2" to 2"
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut seven plate slot slats at 34-1/2" from the 1x2. Cut six reinforcing crosspieces at 34-1/2" and two side rails at 62-3/4" from the 1x4. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to four 8 ft 1x2 and four 10 ft 1x4. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Lay out the slot spacing
steps.body: Mark evenly spaced positions for the seven slats and six crosspieces along both side rails.

steps.title: Assemble the rack
steps.body: Attach the slats and crosspieces between the two side rails at the marked positions, gluing and nailing or screwing each joint.
materials: Brad nails, 1-1/4", or self-tapping star bit screws, 1-1/2" to 2"

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Mount to the wall
steps.body: Mount securely into a wall stud — a fully loaded rack is heavier than it looks.

unresolvedImages.alt: Full length wall mounted plate rack with seven slots
